title: How North Korea Won: Advancing Nuclear Goals and Ties with China
content:
In recent years, North Korea has employed unconventional methods to fund its military ambitions. There was a significant surge in online scams, frauds, and security breaches orchestrated by North Korean cybercriminals. Some individuals even secured jobs with legitimate companies in the United States, posing as remote information technology workers and earning substantial salaries. The regime ensured that this illicitly acquired money was channeled directly into its nuclear weapons program. Consequently, despite facing a severe public health crisis, the country successfully developed tactical nuclear weapons, hypersonic glide vehicles, and new solid-fueled intercontinental ballistic missiles.

With his regime solidifying power at home, the North Korean leader also identified strategic opportunities abroad. Strategic competition between the United States and China was accelerating, and Pyongyang keenly observed Beijing's unease regarding strengthened American alliances in the Indo-Pacific. Taking advantage of this tension, North Korea began voicing enthusiastic support for China and actively opposing United States policies. The regime firmly aligned itself with Beijing's stance on Taiwan, denouncing American military maneuvers in the region as a dangerous provocation.

Furthermore, North Korea actively criticized Western security agreements to strengthen its geopolitical position. It condemned the AUKUS agreement, which provided nuclear-powered submarines to Australia, as an extremely dangerous act. The regime also criticized trilateral summits between Washington, Tokyo, and Seoul, labeling them as a dangerous prelude to an Asian version of NATO. By strategically leveraging the growing divide between global superpowers, North Korea has managed to bolster its defense capabilities and secure crucial diplomatic backing. This approach has allowed the isolated nation to significantly expand its geopolitical influence.
